title: Explain Audio Pipeline
short_answer: Audio pipeline is the mic-to-STT path used before transcript text is written.
text: The audio pipeline opens the mic, reads chunks, adjusts audio, filters noise or silence if enabled, and passes speech to STT. It is the live sound path before text exists.
more: The main audio path uses app/inputfeed_to_text/mic_audio_source.py, optional RNNoise in preprocess/rnnoise_preprocessor.py, optional Silero VAD in vad/silero_vad_gate.py, and optional streaming in streaming/whisper_stream_adapter.py. If words are cut off, tune streaming, VAD, STT_CHUNK_MS, and assembler values.

title: Explain Mic Audio Source
short_answer: Mic Audio Source opens the microphone and returns PCM audio chunks.
text: Mic Audio Source is the part that talks to the microphone. It chooses the Windows WASAPI path or the sounddevice path based on settings.
more: The file is app/inputfeed_to_text/mic_audio_source.py. create_mic_audio_source(...) returns WasapiMicAudioSource when source_backend is windows_wasapi_mic, otherwise SoundDeviceMicAudioSource. It can match WINDOWS_MIC_DEVICE_NAME, resample audio, convert stereo to mono, apply input gain, and attach metadata such as device_name and source_backend.

title: Change Source Backend
short_answer: Source backend chooses the microphone capture implementation.
text: Source backend decides how inputfeed_to_text reads microphone audio. Windows defaults to windows_wasapi_mic, and Linux defaults to sounddevice_mic.
more: INPUTFEED_SOURCE_BACKEND is defined in app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py. mic_audio_source.py uses windows_wasapi_mic for WasapiMicAudioSource and anything else for SoundDeviceMicAudioSource. If the app hears no mic, check INPUTFEED_SOURCE_BACKEND and WINDOWS_MIC_DEVICE_NAME before tuning STT.

title: Explain RNNoise
short_answer: RNNoise is an optional noise reduction step before STT.
text: RNNoise tries to clean microphone audio before it reaches speech recognition. If unavailable or unsupported, the code falls back to raw audio.
more: The implementation is app/inputfeed_to_text/preprocess/rnnoise_preprocessor.py and it is enabled by INPUTFEED_ENABLE_RNNOISE. mic_audio_source.py creates RNNoisePreprocessor and records metadata such as rnnoise_enabled, rnnoise_available, rnnoise_active, rnnoise_backend, rnnoise_error, rnnoise_raw_rms, and rnnoise_denoised_rms. Do not assume RNNoise is active just because it is enabled; check status metadata.

title: Explain Silero VAD
short_answer: Silero VAD is an optional speech gate that decides if audio should be transcribed.
text: Silero VAD helps block silence and noise before STT. It checks RMS, speech segments, and speech duration.
more: The file is app/inputfeed_to_text/vad/silero_vad_gate.py. It is controlled by INPUTFEED_ENABLE_SILERO_VAD plus values like INPUTFEED_MIN_RMS, INPUTFEED_SILERO_THRESHOLD, INPUTFEED_SILERO_MIN_SPEECH_MS, INPUTFEED_SILERO_MIN_SILENCE_MS, INPUTFEED_SILERO_SPEECH_PAD_MS, and INPUTFEED_SILERO_MIN_SEGMENTS. If Silero is unavailable or errors, the code can fall back to rms_only allowing behavior.

title: Explain Streaming Whisper
short_answer: Streaming Whisper buffers recent audio and only emits stable transcript text.
text: Streaming Whisper helps avoid writing unstable partial text too quickly. It waits for candidate text to stay stable before emitting it.
more: The streaming code lives in app/inputfeed_to_text/streaming, mainly whisper_stream_adapter.py and streaming_config.py. Settings include INPUTFEED_USE_STREAMING, INPUTFEED_STREAM_STEP_SECONDS, INPUTFEED_STREAM_OVERLAP_SECONDS, INPUTFEED_STREAM_MAX_BUFFER_SECONDS, INPUTFEED_STREAM_COMMIT_STABLE_PASSES, and INPUTFEED_STREAM_RECENT_SEGMENT_COUNT. If text feels delayed or chopped, check these with VAD and STT_CHUNK_MS.
